content: Multi-provider coding agents commonly fail by: (1) single-slot auth stores that overwrite or conflate provider tokens; (2) routing selected models through the original single backend with the wrong Bearer token; (3) treating ChatGPT/Codex subscription OAuth as equivalent to OpenAI Platform API keys (different billing, quotas, endpoints, feature gates); (4) reusing official Codex client_ids for third-party forks; (5) failing to sync refreshed OAuth tokens to subagents/non-main processes; (6) reauthentication loops when switching models/profiles; (7) partial rebrands that still write stock home dirs and phone home via auto-update/telemetry. Documented in Hermes-agent and OpenClaw issue trackers and OpenAI Codex auth docs.
